pages.title: Project Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to the XML-to-Excel CFDI converter project: what it does, who it is for, and how the two versions relate. Links to child pages for setup and version comparison.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: How to run the tool: prerequisites (Python 3.13, pandas, tkinter), running from source vs. using the pre-built EXE, placing XML files in the correct folder, and reading the output Excel file.

pages.title: Version History and Changelog
pages.purpose: Comparison of Version1 and Version2: what changed between releases, new fields added (Retenciones, CfdiRelacionados, ObjetoImp, Descuento), refactored namespace helpers, and expanded CFDI 3.3/4.0 dual-support.

pages.title: Core Application Logic
pages.purpose: Overview of the main script architecture shared by both versions: the ETL pipeline from XML discovery to Excel output, the tkinter GUI layer, and error handling. Links to child pages for XML parsing and Excel output details.

pages.title: CFDI XML Parsing Pipeline
pages.purpose: Detailed walkthrough of how copiar_xml.py discovers, parses, and extracts data from CFDI XML files: file discovery via os.listdir, namespace handling for CFDI 3.3 (NS33) and 4.0 (NS40), the find_one/findall_any helper functions (Version2), and extraction of every CFDI node (Comprobante, Emisor, Receptor, Conceptos, Impuestos, Traslados, Retenciones, Complemento/TimbreFiscalDigital, CfdiRelacionados).

pages.title: Excel Output Generation
pages.purpose: How extracted data is accumulated into the 'registros' list of dicts, converted to a pandas DataFrame, and written to resultado_cfdi.xlsx with to_excel(index=False). Column naming conventions (XPath-style keys), handling of multi-Concepto invoices, and the output file location logic.

pages.title: GUI and Error Handling
pages.purpose: How the tkinter message boxes are used to inform the user of the working directory, success, and errors. The frozen/non-frozen path detection (sys.frozen), the try/except block with traceback, and the messagebox.showerror pattern.

pages.title: Output Excel Schema
pages.purpose: Description of the columns produced in resultado_cfdi.xlsx: the XPath-style column naming convention, one row per XML file, how multiple Conceptos expand into indexed columns, and which fields come from each CFDI node.

pages.title: Build and Distribution
pages.purpose: Overview of how the Python scripts are packaged into standalone Windows executables using PyInstaller. Links to child pages covering the PyInstaller spec files and the runtime internals.

pages.title: PyInstaller Spec Files
pages.purpose: Explanation of the copiar_xml.spec configuration: Analysis block (entry script, no extra binaries/datas), PYZ archive creation, EXE block settings (name, console=False, UPX compression, no windowed traceback). How to rebuild the EXE from source.

pages.title: PyInstaller Build Artifacts
pages.purpose: What the build/ and dist/ directories contain: Analysis-00.toc (dependency manifest), PYZ-00.toc/pyz (compressed Python modules), PKG-00.toc (full package manifest), EXE-00.toc (binary layout), base_library.zip (standard library), localpycs/ (bootstrap bytecode), warn-*.txt (import warnings), xref-*.html (cross-reference report). Covers both convertidor_cfdi and copiar_xml builds.

pages.title: PyInstaller Runtime Internals
pages.purpose: How the frozen executable loads modules at runtime: the ZlibArchiveReader (pyimod01_archive) for reading PYZ archives, the PyiFrozenFinder/PyiFrozenLoader import machinery (pyimod02_importers), ctypes patching (pyimod03_ctypes), and pywin32 initialization (pyimod04_pywin32). Covers the bootstrap sequence from bootloader to main script.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all domain-specific terms, Spanish-language identifiers, CFDI schema fields, and codebase abbreviations that an onboarding engineer needs to understand. Each entry includes code pointers.
